benchmark_metadata.methodology: Scientific sampling with CLAUDE.md compliance analysis

efficiency_analysis.methodology: Functionality score divided by token cost

rankings.analysis: Highest functionality per token. Clean, focused design.
Good MCP integration but token-heavy for single purpose.
Complex but powerful. Violates complexity limits.
Expensive tokens for simple reflection patterns.
Massive complexity violation. Feature bloat evident.

crimes: Complexity score of 10/10 - DOUBLE the CLAUDE.md limit
534 lines of XML soup - maintenance nightmare
62 thinking blocks - thinking paralysis, not thinking clarity
worst_offender.verdict: This isn't 'intelligent' refactoring, it's complexity masturbation. You've created a 16KB monster that violates every simplicity principle. Martin Fowler would cry.

biggest_surprise.discovery: Actually follows CLAUDE.md principles. Compact, efficient, under complexity limits. This is what good command design looks like.

systemic_issues: 40% of commands violate complexity limits - systemic over-engineering
Average 2,645 tokens per command - expensive for simple automation
XML tag proliferation - 44 tags average per command
Thinking block inflation - more thinking, less doing

immediate_actions.rationale: 100% complexity overage is inexcusable. Split into 3-4 focused commands.

architectural_changes: Establish complexity gates in CI/CD - auto-reject complexity > 5
Token budget per command: max 2000 tokens (saves $0.003 per execution)
Mandatory simplicity review for any command > 300 lines
best_practices: Focus on single responsibility - one command, one job
Question every XML tag - most are cargo cult programming

statistical_confidence.sample_representativeness: High - covers ADHD, thinking, refactoring, generation patterns
statistical_confidence.measurement_accuracy: ±4% for token counts, ±1 for complexity scores

savage_but_fair_conclusion.rationale: Two commands flagrantly violate established complexity limits. Token consumption is 30% above optimal. However, 'generate-thinking-command' proves the team CAN build efficient, compliant commands when they try.
savage_but_fair_conclusion.path_forward: Stop building complexity cathedrals. Start building efficiency engines. Your users' tokens (and sanity) depend on it.
